caption: An educational presentation slide for a course on "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" is shown, focusing on "Tutorial – 4" and the calculation of "Work/unit mass of gas compressed." The slide contains a data table with columns labeled "1," "2," "f," and "g," detailing pressure (p in bar), temperature (T in K), enthalpy (h in J/g), and entropy (s in J/gK). Below the table, the formula $\frac{W'}{m} = T_1(s_1 - s_2) - (h_1 - h_2)$ is applied, showing the calculation $300(4.4-2.75)-(462-430) = 463 \text{ J/g}$. A diagram illustrating a thermodynamic cycle with labels such as $\frac{W'_{rev}}{m}$ and $\frac{W'_{nd}}{m}$ is visible on the left. The bottom of the slide credits "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay" and features an "NPTEL" logo. As the sequence progresses, the final calculation is completed at the bottom right, adding $463 + 23.2 = 486.2 \text{ J/g}$.
